message:
docs(readme): correct squeeze return-type chain (br-szafc / fd90.106)

fd90.106: lines 1131-1132 in DataFrame Introspection claimed:

  let scalar = single_cell_df.squeeze(0)?;  // DataFrame → Scalar

But crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:24938 declares:
  pub fn squeeze(&self, axis: usize) -> Result<Series, Self>

DataFrame::squeeze ALWAYS returns Series, never Scalar. Series::squeeze
(line 5146, no args) returns Result<Scalar, Box<Self>>. To go from
DataFrame to Scalar you must chain through Series.

A user copy-pasting the original line would silently bind 'scalar' to
a Series and the type confusion would cascade.

Fix: split the example into three lines that show:
  - DataFrame → Series via squeeze(1) on column-collapse
  - DataFrame → Series via squeeze(0) on row-collapse
  - Series → Scalar via Series::squeeze() returning Result<Scalar, Box<Series>>

Annotated with the actual return types so readers see the two-step
nature of getting all the way down to a Scalar.

Pure README change. No code impact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
